Overall:
The following processes have been demonstrated to be effective for the reduction of methiocarb from drinking water: chlorine dioxide (>99 oxidation) and radiation processing (26 to 58 oxidation).  Although chlorine dioxide was found to effectively oxidize methiocarb, a concern for increased toxicity is raised with this treatment method due to the formation of the more toxic by-product methiocarb sulfoxide.  Performance of these technologies can vary based on water quality and applied dosages.
